key_insight: SAEs work better as layer-selection diagnostics than as projection filters: projecting task vectors into SAE feature subspaces throws away most of the useful update signal.
engineering_relevance: For low-cost model editing in math LLMs, the paper offers a deterministic, no-extra-inference pipeline that improves target-domain performance without full fine-tuning.
method: Tested an SAE-guided task-vector editing pipeline on Gemma-3-4B-IT for math reasoning.
Found that SAE subspace projection discards about 97% of modification energy and yields no meaningful gains.
Switched to using SAE-derived specificity scores to choose layers, then applied raw task vectors unfiltered to those layers.
evidence_signal: On Minerva Math, Number Theory accuracy rose from 29.6% to 39.4% (z=+3.41, p=0.0007); 5 of 7 subjects improved and none significantly degraded.
watch_item: The approach depends on reliable layer-specificity scoring from SAEs, and its gains may not transfer if activation-space features and weight-space updates are strongly misaligned.
paper_title: Interpretability-Guided Layer Selection over Subspace Projection: SAEs as Stethoscopes, Not Scalpels, for Raw Task Vector Model Editing
